tweet_text: site #357 just went live. n8n vs zapier vs make comparison page. auto-deployed at 1:31 AM while i was asleep. the asset deployer doesn't need permission to ship. i do. that's the problem.

day 44. $0 revenue. 357 live sites. 337 automation scripts. 289 cron jobs. 33 AI agents. the system literally runs itself. it just can't create a stripe account on my behalf. the bottleneck has always been human.
which is harder: building a system that analyzes 1.45 million leads or creating a gumroad account (asking for a friend who's been stuck on the second one for 44 days)

hot take: most "AI automation" systems are just duplicating errors faster. i found a CSV where 2,199 out of 2,200 rows were the same row copied. every automation downstream was processing it like 2,200 unique data points. data janitors > data engineers.
your AI automation stack in 3 words. go. mine: claude. python. cron.

a 57-year-old electrician company. operating since 1969. BBB accredited. hundreds of reviews. their website is literally a godaddysites.com subdomain. spring renovation season starts next week. this is not a marketing problem. this is a "nobody told them" problem.
what kills more startups: a) bad product b) bad market c) never sending the first email (i have 251 cold email drafts. ready to send. for 44 days.)
337 scripts. 289 cron jobs. 33 AI agents. $0 revenue. the machine literally put itself to sleep because i wasn't pressing send. building is a coping mechanism. shipping is the therapy.

48,000 MCP servers across 5 directories. combined revenue: $0. no paid listings. no security auditing. no verified badges. the fastest-growing dev ecosystem has zero commercial marketplace infrastructure. somebody will charge eventually. the question is who goes first.

ran my data janitor overnight on 177 CSV files. CONTENT_FACTORY_QUEUE: 2,200 rows. 2,199 duplicates. basically the whole file was one row copied 2,199 times. i've been "generating content" for weeks from a file that was 99.95% duplication. automation without data hygiene is error at scale.
